rulings.Rulings: Tentative ruling by Judge Ross C. Moody. (1) Co-Successor Trustee's Petition to Bifurcate is DENIED. The Court does not find that bifurcation at this time would necessarily promote "the economy and efficiency of handling the litigation." (CCP 598.) This case has been set for a bench trial on October 2, 2023 out of Department 206. How this case should be managed at the trial level is better left to the assigned trial judge who can, at that time, collaboratively discuss with counsel on how to best manage and try this case. Any request regarding the order in which the issues should be tried should be made to the trial judge. (2) Co-Successor Trustee's Motion to Strike is DENIED. Notwithstanding CCP section 435's requirement that motions to strike be made "within the time allowed to respond to a pleading," the Trustee, who will be referred to as "Respondent" for purposes of the motion, purports to move under CCP section 436, which Respondent contends allows the court "at any time in its discretion" to strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter. Respondent's reliance on section 436 is misplaced. The timing requirements for a motion to strike are laid out in section 435. While the Court has discretion to strike a pleading at any time, it has not indicated any intent to do so in this matter. Rather, it is Respondent who filed the motion, and that motion must comply with the timing requirements under section 435, as it expressly states in section 436 ["The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion ."] The Court declines to exercise its discretion and consider the tardy motion to strike. As noted above, the petition is set for trial on October 2, 2023. All affirmative defenses and collateral estoppel arguments can be addressed to the trial court. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
